node.statement: (Quantum marginal problem application). The set of global quantum states compatible with prescribed marginals (reduced density matrices) on subsystems S_1, ..., S_k is C = {ρ ∈ S(B(H)) : Tr_{H\S_j}(ρ) = ρ_j, j = 1,...,k}. Each marginal constraint Tr_{H\S_j}(ρ) = ρ_j amounts to dim(H_{S_j})² real affine constraints (matching each matrix element of the partial trace). So m = Σ_{j=1}^k dim(H_{S_j})². The main theorem bounds the commutant dimension of extremal compatible states: Σ n_i² ≤ m + 1 = 1 + Σ_j dim(H_{S_j})². For qubit marginals (dim = 2): each marginal contributes 4 constraints, so m = 4k, and the bound is Σ n_i² ≤ 4k + 1.
